head: dense_fg_aux
hypothesis: E-50: Backbone capacity probe — MV3-Small (1.4M) → MV3-Large (3.5M). Does a 2.5x larger backbone in the same architecture family convert dense_fg_aux supervision into better bbox-only inference? All variables held constant vs E-45 except backbone. If pf_mIoU > E-45 (0.8281) by ≥0.01, MV3-Large is a clear upgrade. If marginal (~0.005), the gain may not justify the compute cost. Expected: better feature quality from larger backbone should benefit from rich spatial supervision.
